title: CAU Imphal ORDINANCE 2 of 1995
text: GOVERNMENT OF INDIA MINISTRY OF AGRICULTURE DEPARTMENT OF AGRICULTURAL RESEARCH AND EDUCATION CENTRAL AGRICULTURAL UNIVERSITY IMPHAL : t‘(1AN IPUR 0•R•D• N-A-N C•E Imphal,22nd March, 1995 No.2 of 1995° No. VC/ CAU/ 114( ESTABL ISH)/94 sanction Wherais--the—o of the Central Agricultural University Scheme under the .VI I Ith Five-Year Plan, as communicated bye the Joint Secretary, Department of Agricultural Research and Education and Secretary, Indian Council of Agricultural Research, vide letter numb.er 1(2) 92-Plan( Part- I I I ) , dated the 13th October, 1993, prov ides for the establ hinent of the Central Agricultural University with headquaters at imphal and seven constituent College spread over six states-College Of Agriculture (Manipur) , College of Veterinary Sciences(Mizoram) , College of Horticulture and Forestry ( Arunachal Pradesh), College of Fisheries (Tripura), College of Agricultural Engineering and Post-Harvest Technology (Sikkim) , College of Home Science and College of Post-Graduate Studies (Meghalaya) . Whereas the aforesaid sanction also provides for creation and the filling up of both academic and non-academic/supporting staff as provtded therein to run the university and its constituent colleges with the necessary mod i f i cat ion (s), if any , con s d erect necessary by the Central Agricultural University. And whereas th Government of India in the Iepartment of Agricultural Research and Education/ Indian Council of A ricul tura I Research has approved to make an Ordinance regarding the appoint- ments and emoluments of employees other than those for whom -- 1 of 30 -- iv•DAvi' jmA Cderld‘ provision has been made in the Statutes of tile Genii- al Aninciiii University, I alpha I videletter No . 2-1 / 94 -EcIn . I V , dated 14th March i Now, therefore, in ekerci se of the powers conferred by Sub-section (2) read with clause ( j ) of Sub-section ( 1 ) of Section 27 of the tCentral Agricultural University Act, 1992 (No. 40 of 1992) and with previous approval of the Central Government, as aforesaid,I ,the Vice-Chancellor of the Central Agricultural University, Imphal, do hereby make this -first Ordinance and notify that the various non-teaching positions of the University as 'contained in Appendix-I sh311 carry the pay scales and be filled up following the guidelines contained in Appendix-II of this ordinance and the procedures as laid down hereunder. 1. The different sanctione.d non-teaching position_ of the University with the scale of • pay shall be one as contained In Appendix-I of this Ordinance. The V ice-Chancel lor,however, sha I I have the authority to transfer one or more pocition( 5.1 wi th or without the person occupying the. said position( s) , from one unit/ constituent college to another in the interest of the University work, 2. The different non-teaching position of the University shall stand grouped and categorised as follows : S I , No. Group' Category' Pay Scale 1. Group 'A' AA Post(s) in the pay scale Rs, . 4500-7300/- AB -do- Rs.. 4500-5700/ - AC -do- Rs. 3700-5700/ - AD -do- Rs.. 3000-4500/ - AE -do- 2. R. 2200-4000/ - Group 'B' BA Pos I s) in the pay scale Rs. 2000-3500/- RR -do- Rs. 1640-2900 / - 3 Group 'C' CA Posts( s) in the pay scale Rs. 1400-2300/ - CB • -do- Rs. 1200-2040/ - CC . -do- Rs. 950-1400/ - 4 . Group 'D' DA Post( s) in the pay scale • • Rs. °7s0-950/- ---------------------------------------------------------------------- -- 2 of 30 -- Colleges appointed , sd«itt - t ( 1 in nil ochres and u ncler the University shall be in one cadre and personnel on a position in a particular cadre could transfarred tty. from one unit/campus to another by the University author All q. posts carrying pay scale of R si220-11009- and above, the appointing authority shall be the Vice-Chancellor with-the approval of the Board of Management of the University and for post carrying grade lower than Rs. 2200-4009 - the Vice-Chancellor shall he the approving and the appointing authority. 5. All posts in the Central Agricultural •University shall be filled by direct recruitment through open advertisement, except when there is a privision for filling - them by promotion from amongst the employees working in the Central Agricultural University on an immediately- lower-position as mentioned in the recruitment guide- <. I um .0 con to Inc(' in Appendeex-H dud there Shall he 0 Select ion Lommi t tee consisting of the members, as stated immediately here (inner, for making recommendation to the respective authority for appointment to the post( si to be filed up. . SI .tio. Group of i posts ' Selection Committee Group 'A' 1) Vice-Chancellor 2) One Di rector and one Dean (to be non ina teci by V .C.) 3) Two experts not in the service of the Central Agricu I tural University from the panel of four names approved by the Board of Management. At least one of them must be present - Chairman Member Me mh e r Member Secretary 4) Registrar -- 3 of 30 -- 3. Group ' I 1 One r Pc t Or ( tohe - Chairman nominated by the Vice-Chancel lor ) 2) One Dean to be Member nominated by the Vice-Chancellor 3) Registrar - Member Secretary ) One expert - Member depending upon the nature of the posts to be nominated by the Vice-Chancellor 1 ) Di rector/Dean to be - Chairman nominated by V ice- Chancel lor 2) Registrar or his Member. nominee 3') One Officer from outside University to be nominated by the V ice- Chancel tor. Member Notwithstanding anything contained in tThe provisions made above, the Vice-Chancellor may nominate an officer of the University to preside over the meetings of the Selection Comittee in respect, of Group 'A' posts. Also if the posts of Directors are vacant or, for some reason he is not in a position to preside over the meetings of the Selection Committee in respect of Group 'B' positions the Vice-Chancellor shall normally preside over the meet ings( s) . For similar reason the meetings of the Select ion Committee in respect of' the Group 'C' positions shall he presided by one of the Deans to nominated by the Vice-Chancellor, 5. The under-mentioned procedures shall be followed by the Selection Commi ttee( s) while evaluating the candidates cal led for interview and for making recommendation( s) Niwiv ,trait 9o1+ -- 4 of 30 -- N. awer,ett 5-te' . Each Selection Committee shall decide its own methods(s) and procedure( s) for assessment of the suitability and making recommend- ation of thee candidates on the basis of the guide! i nes, i f any, issued by the University and the qualifications and other eligibility criteria as contained -in Appendix-II of this Ordinance. i i . Of the total posts to' be filled by direct recruitment in a parti- cular cadre (Post wise) in a single calender year 22.5 per cent shall be reserved for the scheduled caste and scheduled tribe candi- dates possessing the prescribed qualification( s) and experience and 27 per cent shall be reserved for other Backward C la ssess (0BCs) as per the Govt. of Indian quid lines. iii) In case of promotion, where test or personal interview is not considered necessary, panel may be drawn up by the Selection Commi ttee on the basis of asessement of the records of works and of conduct of the employment ( s) concerned. iv) If the service of employees working in the service of Central State Government of Public Sector Undertaking, statutory bodies inclu- ding ICAR and 'agricultural universities, etc.- are taken on deputation, - their services shat t) be governed by the standard terms and conditions of deputation as applicable in the Govt. of India, 7. Procedure for Temporary Appointment : Temporary appointment(s) and appointment(s) for a fixed period shall be made by the concerned authority in the manner pres- cribed below . • (a) If the temporary vacancy is for a duration longer than one year, it shall be filled on - the recommended of the Selection Committee in accordance with the protedure indicated in the forgoing clauses of this Ordinance. Provided that if the Vice-Chancellor is satisfied that in the interest of work it is necessary to fill a vacancy immediately, the -- 5 of 30 -- h1. bmr, ,, gpb, -24 6 appointment (s) may be made on adhoe basis for a period not ex cee- ding five months in respect of Group 'C' and 'D' posts only on the recommendation of a local Selection Committee consisting of Director/Dean, Registrar or his nominee and one nominee of the Vice-Chancellor, No extension in service beyond five months shall be_ granted to the employee( s) appointed by this procedure. (b) No employee appointed purely on temporary basis shall be continued in. service beyond five months period unless he is recomm- ended for appointment on the said post or any position by the appr- opriate Selection Comi ttee 8. Emoluments of the employees and terms and conditions of service : C Employees of the Central Agricultural University will be entitled to the pay scales • and other allowances prescri bed by the University from time to time and they will be governed by the Service Rules of the University. S. Age of Retirement Every non- teaching and supporting employee of the Univer- sity shall retire on the la-st day of the month in which he/she shall be attaining the age of 58 (fifty-eight) years. 10, Probation All newly appointed /promoted personel initially shall be on probation for a period of two years. If the performance of any probationer is not found satisfactory the appointing authority by specific order may extend the period of probation or his/her service may be dispensed with or he/she may be reverted to his/her pre- vious position. 11. Age limits for Direct Recruitment The minimum agelimit for appointment to any position in the University shall be 18 (eighteen) years and the maximum aoe -- 6 of 30 -- iimi t 35 I thirty five) years. The maximum age limit shall, however , he relaxable for SG/ST candidates as per the Central Government rules and for thOse already in Govrnment/Semi-Government/ :GAR /Universi ty Service. 12. Every duly appointed regular employees of the Manipur Agri- cultural College, Imphal who has been in employment in before the date of take over th College by the Col 1e9 the Central Agriculturat University (2 2.11.93) and has become the employee of the Univer-. sity from the said date shall hold. office with service protection under the University s with such. rights and privileges as contri bu tat) provident fund, gratuity, leave, medical benefits etc.to him/her immediate) admissible y before as admi the take over of the gs id col lege by the Central- Agricultural University, The employee shall el so con:lime to be in the service of the Central Agricultural University with their old designations with grades/pay scales enjoyed by them -immediately before the take over of the college, if their grades of pay immedi- ately before the take over of thee col lege . are different from the one_ (s) provided under this Oriclinance, they shall continue to work in their respective grades as "Personel Grades" till they, an the basis of direct recruitment/promotion, happen to occupy a different position, In the latter case, the designation privileges and term ms and condi- tions of service shall Se same as Prescribed for the employees of the Central Agricultural University. 13 In all matters, not provided in the ordinane, till the farming of the detailed service condi t ioins for the employee of the Central Agricultural University, the conditions of the service of the employees of the University shall temporari I I y be governed •by the rules applic- able to the employees of the ICAR working in the North-Eastern Region. After enforcement of the new service rules of the Central Agric- ultural University, the Service conditions of the Central Agricultural University shall be governed by • the rew rules automatically, employees of the -- 7 of 30 -- 14. The privision (s) of this Ordinance shall come into effect from • the date of its publication in the official Gazette. (M.P.
